16.text: That not only my soul after this life shall be immediately taken up to Christ its Head; but also that this my body, raised by the power of Christ, shall again be united with my soul and made like unto

Finally, we believe, according to the Word of God, when the time appointed by the Lord (which is unknown to all creatures) is come and the number of the elect complete, that our Lord Jesus Christ will

Christ is to be exalted in his coming again to judge the world, in that he, who was unjustly judged and condemned by wicked men, shall come again at the last day in great power, and in the full manife

3.text: That all unchastity is condemned of God, and that therefore we must with all our hearts detest the same, and live chastely and temperately, whether in holy wedlock or in single life.

17.text: At the last day, such as are found alive shall not die, but be changed: and all the dead shall be raised up, with the selfsame bodies, and none other (although with different qualities), which shall b

14.text: The souls of believers are at their death made perfect in holiness, and do immediately pass into glory; and their bodies, being still united to Christ, do rest in their graves till the resurrection.

4.text: The seventh commandment requireth the preservation of our own and our neighbour's chastity, in heart, speech, and behaviour.

13.text: We receive all these books, and these only, as holy and canonical, for the regulating, founding, and establishing of our faith; believing without any doubt all things contained in them, not so much be

The authority of the Holy Scripture, for which it ought to be believed and obeyed, dependeth not upon the testimony of any man or church; but wholly upon God (who is truth itself), the author thereof:
